title: Organise posts and files so learners can find things
staff_situations: How do I organise posts and files so learners can find things?
underlying_needs: A consistent, predictable structure so learners spend less time searching and more time working
A visual pattern learners can recognise at a glance, alongside clear text labels

The same resource can appear in more than one place, as long as permissions are set correctly and it's still easy to find. This isn't duplication for its own sake — it means a learner can find what they need from wherever they're looking
digital_practices: colour-coding by day, session or recurring topic
channel structure by topic or unit

accessibility: Colour-coding is paired with clear text labels so meaning never depends on colour alone

Consistent, predictable structure makes it easier to find your way around if you use a screen reader — software that reads the screen aloud — or switch access — a way of controlling a device without a mouse or touchscreen
inclusive_practice: A well-organised, colour-coded space benefits every learner, not only those who ask where to find something
Means staff spend less time repeating the same directions to individual learners
